headline: Explore NASA's InSight Mission on Mars
byline: The New York Times · Augmented Reality · 2018

beats.caption: THE INSIGHT LANDER is NASA's newest mission to Mars, set to land on Nov. 26, 2018. It will study the deep structure of the planet.
THE CURIOSITY ROVER has been exploring and drilling in Gale Crater since 2012.
THE PHOENIX LANDER touched down near Mars' north pole in 2008, and found water ice under the surface.
THE OPPORTUNITY ROVER roamed Mars from 2004 until a 2018 dust storm obscured its solar panels and cut its power.
THE SPIRIT ROVER roamed Mars from 2004 to 2009, when it became stuck in a patch of soft ground.
THE PATHFINDER MISSION landed in 1997 and carried Sojourner, the first Martian rover.
THE VIKING LANDERS, 1 and 2, both carried seismometers in the 1970s but were unable to definitively detect marsquakes.
EARLY MARS. An artist's conception of the young planet, before its water was lost to space or froze into the ground.
INSIGHT ON THE SURFACE. Unlike the rovers, InSight stays put — a stationary platform built to listen to the planet itself.
SEISMOMETER (SEIS). Lowered to the ground under a chainmail-skirted dome, it detects vibrations from marsquakes and meteorite impacts.
HEAT FLOW PROBE (HP³). A self-hammering probe drives about 16 feet into the soil to measure heat welling up from the planet's core.
RADIO EXPERIMENT (RISE). Antennas on the deck let Earth track how Mars wobbles as it orbits the sun, revealing the size of its core.
